title: Rework constellation lines to form recognizable geometric topologies

tests.description: TOPOLOGIES array contains exactly 3 topology definitions (tetrahedron, octahedron, icosahedron)
Tetrahedron has 4 vertices and 6 edges
Octahedron has 6 vertices and 12 edges
Icosahedron has 12 vertices and 30 edges
All edge indices are within vertex array bounds for each topology
assertions: For each topology, every edge [a, b] satisfies a >= 0 && a < vertices.length && b >= 0 && b < vertices.length

tests.description: Each topology vertex has exactly 3 components (x, y, z)
assertions: For each topology, every vertex array has length 3

tests.description: pickTopologies returns correct count with seeded determinism
assertions: Two calls with same seed produce identical selections

tests.description: pickTopologies with count 0 returns empty array
pickTopologies allows repeats (count can exceed TOPOLOGIES.length)
generateTopologyInstances produces non-overlapping instances (minimum separation enforced)
assertions: For all pairs of instances, Euclidean distance between positions >= minimum separation threshold

tests.description: generateTopologyInstances returns correct number of instances (up to requested count)
generateTopologyInstances is seeded-deterministic (same seed produces same layout)
assertions: Two calls with createPRNG('same-seed') produce identical instance positions and quaternions

tests.description: generateTopologyInstances different seeds produce different layouts
assertions: Instances from seed 'alpha' differ from instances from seed 'beta' in position values

tests.description: flattenInstances world-space positions differ from unit positions (transform applied)
assertions: Flattened positions are not equal to raw unit vertex coordinates
At least some Z values are non-zero (3D verification)

tests.description: flattenInstances edge remapping is correct (offset by cumulative vertex count)
assertions: For second instance, edge indices are offset by first instance's vertex count
All edge indices are within bounds of total flattened vertex count

tests.description: flattenInstances total vertex count equals sum of per-instance vertex counts
flattenInstances total edge count equals sum of per-instance edge counts
Instance quaternions are unit length (valid rotations)
assertions: For each instance, sqrt(q[0]^2 + q[1]^2 + q[2]^2 + q[3]^2) ≈ 1.0

tests.description: Instance scale values are within expected range (0.3–0.7)
Instance positions are within spread radius
assertions: For each instance, sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2) <= spreadRadius

tests.description: Each topology instance's vertices form a tight cluster (bounding sphere check)
assertions: For each instance, all transformed vertices are within instance.scale * 2 of instance.position (generous bound)

tests.description: flattenInstances positions contain no NaN or Infinity values
assertions: Every value in positions Float32Array is finite (Number.isFinite)

testsToRemove.reason: Proximity-based connection logic replaced by topology edges; proximityThreshold config no longer exists
Configurable proximity threshold replaced by topology-based edge tables
Distance-based alpha ordering is no longer relevant; edges are topology-defined, not distance-sorted
maxConnections config semantics change; connection count is now driven by topology instance count and edge tables
Dense point cloud proximity runaway is no longer possible; topology edges are fixed per definition
Null positions handling changes; system now generates its own vertices internally
Empty positions handling changes; system generates vertices internally
Fewer than 2 points check no longer relevant; system generates its own geometry from topology definitions
proximityThreshold=0 boundary test no longer relevant; config field removed
uProximityThreshold uniform may be removed or repurposed; proximity-based modulation replaced by topology structure

testsToKeep.note: init() adds LineSegments mesh — still valid, update to pass maxTopologyInstances config
BufferGeometry with position attribute — still valid
aDistance attribute present — still valid (topology edges have distances)
ShaderMaterial with vertex/fragment shaders — still valid
Transparent blending, depthWrite disabled — still valid
draw() does not throw — still valid

testsToModify.changes: Update to use new config shape (maxTopologyInstances instead of proximityThreshold). Remove generateMockPositions dependency; pass dummy positions since system generates its own.
Rewrite to test maxTopologyInstances tier scaling instead of maxConnections proximity scaling. Low config with fewer instances should produce fewer vertices than high config.
Determinism now depends on seed, not just same positions. Update to verify same seed produces same topology layout.

testsToAdd.description: Topology instances are generated with correct vertex counts per topology type (4/6/12)
assertions: Total active vertex count accounts for topology-specific vertex counts per instance
Vertex count is a multiple of 2 (LineSegments pairs)

testsToAdd.description: Edge count matches topology definitions (not proximity-based)
assertions: Total line segments = sum of edges across all instances (each edge = 2 vertices)

testsToAdd.description: Total line segment count is capped by maxConstellationSegments from quality config
assertions: When maxConnections is set low, activeVertexCount / 2 <= maxConnections

testsToAdd.description: Instance positions are seeded-deterministic (same seed produces same layout)
assertions: Two createConstellationLines with same seed config produce identical activeVertexCount
Position attribute arrays are identical for same seed

testsToAdd.description: Instance count respects maxTopologyInstances from config
assertions: Config with maxTopologyInstances: 3 produces fewer vertices than maxTopologyInstances: 15
Config with maxTopologyInstances: 0 produces no geometry (no LineSegments added to scene)

testsToAdd.description: With maxTopologyInstances: 0, no geometry is created and no mesh is added
Each topology instance's vertices are recognizably clustered in 3D space (bounding sphere per instance)
assertions: Position attribute contains multiple distinct spatial clusters
Vertices within each cluster are closer to each other than to vertices in other clusters

testsToAdd.description: Positions parameter is accepted but ignored (system generates its own vertices)
assertions: init(scene, arbitraryPositions, params) produces geometry independent of positions content
init(scene, new Float32Array(0), params) still produces geometry when maxTopologyInstances > 0

testsToAdd.description: aDistance attribute is present on topology edges with values in [0, 1] range
assertions: All values in aDistance array are >= 0 and <= 1

testsToAdd.description: aRandom attribute is present with itemSize 3 for per-vertex noise
Electric arc mode still works with topology-generated edges
assertions: With enableElectricArc: true and arcSubdivisions > 1, aEdgeParam and aEdgeTangent attributes are present
Active vertex count is greater than non-arc mode (subdivision increases vertices)

testsToAdd.description: Different seeds produce different topology layouts
assertions: Position arrays from different seeds are not identical

testsToAdd.description: Topology vertices exist in 3D (not coplanar, Z values vary across instances)
assertions: Position array contains at least 3 distinct Z values

testsToAdd.description: QualityProfile includes maxTopologyInstances field
assertions: Field exists on low, medium, and high tier profiles

testsToAdd.description: Low tier has maxTopologyInstances: 0 (constellation topology disabled)
Medium tier has maxTopologyInstances: 8
High tier has maxTopologyInstances: 15
extractSystemConfig('constellation') returns topology-related fields
scaleQualityProfile respects COUNT_MINIMUMS for maxTopologyInstances (minimum 0, not 3)
assertions: Scaling low tier by 0.1 still results in maxTopologyInstances >= 0
maxTopologyInstances can be scaled to 0 (fully disabled) unlike other counts that have positive minimums

testsToAdd.description: Tier progression: maxTopologyInstances increases from low to medium to high

generateMockPositions.note: Existing helper in constellationLines.test.ts; retained for backward compatibility but less critical since system generates its own vertices
generateMockPositions.change: Keep as-is for tests that still pass positions to init()

makeFrame.note: Existing helper in constellationLines.test.ts; no changes needed

defaultParams.note: Existing VisualParams default; no changes needed
